speed limit.answer: The national speed limit on a motorway is 70mph.

seat belt.answer: You must wear a seat belt unless exempt.

mobile phone.answer: It is illegal to use a handheld mobile phone while driving.

traffic lights.answer: Red means stop, amber means stop if safe, green means go.

motorway.answer: Motorways are identified by blue signs.

drink driving.answer: Never drive after consuming alcohol above the legal limit.

stopping distance.answer: Stopping distance is thinking distance plus braking distance.

blind spot.answer: Always check blind spots before changing lanes.

speed limit motorway.answer: The national speed limit on a motorway is 70 mph unless signs show otherwise.

speed limit dual carriageway.answer: The national speed limit for cars on a dual carriageway is 70 mph.

speed limit single carriageway.answer: The national speed limit for cars on a single carriageway is 60 mph.

built up area speed limit.answer: The speed limit is usually 30 mph unless signs indicate otherwise.

speeding.answer: Speeding increases the risk of collisions and reduces reaction time.

give way sign.answer: You must give priority to traffic on the main road.

stop sign.answer: You must stop completely before proceeding.

no entry sign.answer: Vehicles must not enter beyond this sign.

national speed limit sign.answer: This sign indicates that the national speed limit applies.

warning signs.answer: Warning signs are usually triangular with a red border.

zebra crossing.answer: Give way to pedestrians waiting to cross.

pelican crossing.answer: Pedestrians control the crossing using push-button signals.

puffin crossing.answer: Sensors detect pedestrians and control the crossing time.

toucan crossing.answer: Both pedestrians and cyclists may use this crossing.

school crossing patrol.answer: You must stop when instructed by the patrol.

dual carriageway.answer: A road with a central reservation separating traffic.

roadworks.answer: Obey temporary signs, signals and speed limits.

hard shoulder.answer: Use only in emergencies unless signs indicate otherwise.

one way street.answer: Traffic may travel in one direction only.

country roads.answer: Be prepared for sharp bends, animals and slow-moving vehicles.

roundabout.answer: Give priority to traffic approaching from the right.

mini roundabout.answer: Drive around the central marking and give way to traffic from the right.

box junction.answer: Do not enter unless your exit road is clear.

t junction.answer: Give way to traffic on the main road.

crossroads.answer: Watch carefully for traffic from all directions.

tyre pressure.answer: Check tyre pressure regularly for safety and fuel efficiency.

headlights.answer: Use headlights at night and when visibility is poor.

fog lights.answer: Use them when visibility is seriously reduced.

indicators.answer: Use indicators to signal your intentions to other road users.

brakes.answer: Ensure your brakes are working correctly before driving.

motorway signs.answer: Motorways are identified by blue signs.

joining motorway.answer: Use the slip road to build up speed before merging.

leaving motorway.answer: Move into the left lane in good time and follow the exit signs.

emergency areas.answer: On smart motorways, emergency areas are provided for breakdowns.

following distance.answer: Use the two-second rule in dry conditions and at least double it in wet conditions.

icy roads.answer: Increase your following distance and drive more slowly.

fatigue.answer: Stop and rest if you feel tired while driving.

mirrors.answer: Check mirrors regularly and before changing speed or direction.

children in cars.answer: Children must use an appropriate child seat until legally exempt.

overtaking.answer: Only overtake when it is safe and legal to do so.

tram crossing.answer: Take extra care and obey all signs and signals.

road markings.answer: Road markings provide important information and guidance to drivers.

lane closure.answer: Move into an open lane safely and in good time.

rural roads.answer: Be aware of hidden junctions, bends and animals.

amber traffic light.answer: Amber means stop unless it is unsafe to do so.

green traffic light.answer: Green means you may proceed if the way is clear.

flashing amber beacon.answer: Warns of a hazard ahead such as a school crossing.

left turn signal.answer: Signal in good time before turning left.

parking on a hill.answer: Apply the handbrake and turn the wheels appropriately.

windscreen.answer: Keep the windscreen clean and free from obstructions.

engine oil.answer: Check engine oil regularly and maintain the correct level.

battery warning light.answer: It may indicate a charging system fault.

tyre tread.answer: The minimum legal tread depth is 1.6 mm across the central three-quarters of the tyre.
